segments.text: Would you buy a luxury product made out of toxic river waste? Because this company just figured out how to turn pollution into a billiondoll opportunity. Every day millions of
flowers are dumped into the Ganga. They rot, they release pesticide and kill the river. So fool decided to harvest them.
But selling trash to people is hard and usually eco-friendly means brown and boring. So look at what they did with the branding. They removed all the traditional religious imagery and
replaced it with bright neon and modern art. They made it feel like a premium lifestyle choice, not just a ritual. But the real magic isn't just the incense. It's what they did next to the flowers.
They discovered a way to grow leather-like material directly on top of flower waste. It's called fleather. It feels like animal skin, but it's entirely vegan and grows from the very
trash that was killing the river. That's how you turn a national disaster into global treasure with cry for
